conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. I've reviewed the eligibility rules. To help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I've got one child - my son Alex. He's 15 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask:

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Bristol.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great. Now I need to understand a bit more about Alex's situation.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training, or has he started paid work for 24 hours or more a week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's still at school - just doing his GCSEs at the moment. He doesn't have a job or anything like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Is Alex currently in hospital or care for an extended period, or does he live with someone else (such as a friend or relative)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. He's just at home with me, healthy and well thankfully.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just one final question:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 15 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you in the UK (Bristol)
- He's in approved education (doing his GCSEs)
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You meet all the eligibility criteria. You're responsible for Alex as he lives with you, and he's under 16 and in education.

**Please note:** If either you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim Child Benefit even if the tax charge applies.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit through the government website. If you have any questions about making a claim, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for assistance.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK (Bristol), is still in approved education (doing his GCSEs at school), is not in hospital or care, lives at home with you, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. Alex meets all these criteria, so you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They live in the UK, Alex is 15 years old and under 16, lives at home with the claimant, is in approved education (school), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.

According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. Alex meets all these criteria, so you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. They live in the UK, Alex is 15 years old and under 16, lives at home with the claimant, is in approved education (school), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. All eligibility criteria are met.
